discrepancies.what: Pade-cell OK count 39/49 per rep (156/196 total) vs slot 184 prompt expectation "expect well-formed Pade construction throughout" (i.e. 49/49 per rep matching 092 baseline)
discrepancies.observation: The 10 NO_POLE_NEAR_2 cells per rep are concentrated at small (N+M) <= 16, specifically the triangle: (6,6),(6,8),(6,10),(6,12),(8,6),(8,8),(8,10),(10,6),(10,8),(12,6). These cells produce well-formed Pade approximants (no RANK_LOSS, no PADE_FAILED) but the Pade denominator does not have a physical root in the eligibility window (positive real, |Im/|r|| < 5%, in (0, 3]).
discrepancies.interpretation: The K_LEAD=25 subtraction at small n contaminates the Borel-coefficient series (see UF-184-1); the contamination shifts the Pade pole structure away from physical u in [1.5, 2.5] toward generic structural poles outside the eligibility window. Slot 092's raw a_n produces structurally cleaner Pade approximants at this regime because no high-K polynomial-tail contamination is injected.
discrepancies.downstream_impact: Strengthens HALT_A_RESIDUAL_PATTERN_REPRODUCED conclusion: not only does subtraction fail to improve convergence, it actively degrades Pade structure at the small-(N,M) regime.

discrepancies.what: pole_u and dist_to_2 columns are nearly identical across all 4 reps at matching (N,M) cells -- 4 to 6 significant-figure agreement despite the 4 reps having genuinely distinct zeta_star values (V_quad=QL15=2.309, QL05=4.0, QL09=2.828)
discrepancies.observation: Example at (N=8, M=18): dist_to_2 = 0.00332834306311 (V_quad) vs 0.00332834312241 (QL15) vs 0.0033089290712 (QL05) vs 0.00332834281879 (QL09) -- the first 3 agree to 8 digits; QL05 differs at 4th sig fig (which IS the rep with the distinct zeta_star=4.0). Pattern repeats across most cells.
discrepancies.interpretation: The subtracted Borel-coefficient series b_n_residual at small n (n in [0, 60]) is dominated by polynomial-tail contamination C * Gamma(n) * zeta**(-n) * a_25/n^25, scaled by zeta^n / Gamma(n+1). The zeta**(-n) * zeta^n cancels exactly, leaving the residual essentially rep-independent in its dominant Pade-input structure -- only the C amplitude differs. Hence near-identical pole/distance columns across reps. Rep-distinguishing information is LOST.
discrepancies.downstream_impact: Documents a methodological limit on this subtraction quadrant: K_LEAD=25 at small n destroys rep-specificity. This is itself substrate evidence for slot 156 verdict V0+(defended): the M8b axis is closed-by-methodology not closed-by-data; the four quadrants exhaust available Pade methodology in this convention.

discrepancies.what: Stage-1 fit wallclock at dps=200 with Gamma-by-recurrence optimization (4272 s total / ~1018 s per rep) is faster than T37M's reported ~1700 s per rep at the same dps but slower than the 30 s/rep estimate motivating the optimization
discrepancies.observation: Performance budget for stage-1 fit: V_quad 973 s, QL15 1022 s, QL05 1020 s, QL09 1161 s. Total ~71 min vs T37M's projected ~112 min at dps=200 (28 min/rep extrapolated from dps=100 cost-cut commentary).
discrepancies.interpretation: The Gamma-by-recurrence optimization succeeded in eliminating per-call gamma(n_mp) overhead, but the residual cost is dominated by mp.qr() on the (1401, 25) matrix at dps=200. The 60% improvement vs T37M's projection is meaningful but not the order-of-magnitude speedup the optimization could yield if QR were also accelerated. Documented for future fire planning.
discrepancies.downstream_impact: Useful timing data for future T2-EXECUTOR fires that adopt the stage-1 polynomial-correction methodology. The 092 + 184 small-(N,M) regime is now established as a ~1.5-hour wallclock budget at dps=200/300.
